title: Plausible Analytics Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Plausible Analytics** is a privacy-friendly, lightweight, open-source web-analytics product exposing a REST API at `https://plausible.io/api/v1` (cloud) or `https://<self-hosted>/api/v1`. This connector — `PlausibleConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "plausible"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the three operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Stats | `/stats/aggregate`, `/stats/timeseries`, `/stats/breakdown`, `/stats/realtime/visitors` | KPI snapshots, time-bucketed series, paginated dimension breakdowns, live visitor counts |
| Sites Provisioning | `/sites` | List, get, create, update, delete tracked sites |
| Goals | `/sites/{site_id}/goals` | List + create event/page conversion goals |
| Events | `/events` | Anonymous pageview + custom-event ingestion (no Bearer; identity from User-Agent) |

The connector materialises per-site snapshot documents into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{site_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, honours `Retry-After`), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PlausibleHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Plausible API key

1. Sign in to https://plausible.io (or your self-hosted instance).
2. Open **Settings → API Keys → New API key**.
3. Name the key (e.g. `Shielva`) and click **Create**.
4. Copy the API key value — Plausible shows it only once.
5. Note the **site domain** (e.g. `example.com`) you want to connect — that is the `default_site_id`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Plausible Analytics**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Leave blank for cloud (`https://plausible.io`); set to `https://<host>` for self-hosted |
| `default_site_id` (string, optional) | Plausible site domain (e.g. `example.com`) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 600 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `PlausibleHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()`. When `default_site_id` is set, it issues `GET /stats/realtime/visitors?site_id={default_site_id}` — a cheap probe. When it is blank, it falls back to `GET /sites`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → retried with exponential backoff.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Plausible REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry — until the key is revoked from the dashboard.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as **`Bearer <api_key>`** for Stats + Sites + Goals endpoints.
- The **Events** endpoint is anonymous — identity is derived from the `User-Agent` header. The connector deliberately omits `Authorization` on `/events` so the IP/UA are the only identification surface (matches Plausible's privacy posture).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `PlausibleHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Plausible endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured key — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /stats/realtime/visitors?site_id={default_site_id}` (or `GET /sites` fallback). |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the key lives in the encrypted connector config. |

### Header contract

Stats + Sites + Goals:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

Events:

```
User-Agent:   <forwarded visitor UA>
Content-Type: application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Authorization Bearer value |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://plausible.io/api/v1`) | Override for self-hosted instance |
| `default_site_id` | string | no | `self.default_site_id` | Default Plausible site domain (e.g. `example.com`) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 600) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://plausible.io/api/v1"
```

`base_url` is normalised to always end in `/api/v1`, so callers may pass either form (`https://plausible.io` or `https://plausible.io/api/v1`).

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PlausibleConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PlausibleHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `PlausibleHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo with `access_token=<api_key>`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /stats/realtime/visitors?site_id={default_site_id}` (preferred) or `GET /sites` (fallback).

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Materialises a 30-day aggregate + realtime snapshot as a `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{default_site_id}"`) and ingests it via `ingest_document`.

children.title: Stats
children.content:
### `async aggregate(site_id, *, period="30d", date=None, metrics=None, filters=None, compare=None) -> Dict`
`GET /stats/aggregate`. Default metrics: `visitors,pageviews,bounce_rate,visit_duration`.

### `async timeseries(site_id, *, period="30d", date=None, metrics=None, filters=None, interval="date") -> Dict`
`GET /stats/timeseries`. `interval`: `date` (default), `month`, or `hour`.

### `async breakdown(site_id, *, period="30d", date=None, property="event:page", metrics=None, filters=None, page=1, limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /stats/breakdown`. Adds a `normalized` sibling key splitting each row into `{dimension, metrics}`.

### `async realtime_visitors(site_id) -> Dict`
`GET /stats/realtime/visitors`. Wraps Plausible's integer payload as `{visitors: <n>}`.

Spec aliases also exposed: `get_aggregate`, `get_timeseries`, `get_breakdown`, `get_realtime_visitors`.

children.title: Events (anonymous)
children.content:
### `async record_pageview(domain, url, *, user_agent=..., screen_width=None, referrer=None) -> Dict`
`POST /events` with `name="pageview"`. No `Authorization` header — identity from `User-Agent`.

### `async record_custom_event(domain, name, url, *, user_agent=..., props=None, referrer=None) -> Dict`
`POST /events` with the custom `name` and optional `props` map.

### `async send_event(domain, name, url, ...) -> Dict`
Spec-aligned alias for `record_custom_event`.

children.title: Sites Provisioning
children.content:
### `async list_sites() -> Dict`
`GET /sites`.

### `async get_site(site_id) -> Dict`
`GET /sites/{site_id}`.

### `async create_site(domain, timezone="UTC") -> Dict`
`POST /sites`. Body: `{domain, timezone}`.

### `async update_site(site_id, *, timezone=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /sites/{site_id}`. Body: `{timezone?}`.

### `async delete_site(site_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /sites/{site_id}`.

children.title: Goals
children.content:
### `async list_goals(site_id) -> Dict`
`GET /sites/{site_id}/goals`.

### `async create_goal(site_id, goal_type, *, event_name=None, page_path=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sites/{site_id}/goals`. Validates that `event` goals carry `event_name` and `page` goals carry `page_path` before send — raises `PlausibleAPIError(status_code=400)` otherwise.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PlausibleError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PlausibleAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── PlausibleBadRequestError          # 400
├── PlausibleNotFound                 # 404
├── PlausibleRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
├── PlausibleNetworkError             # transport + 5xx
└── PlausibleAPIError                 # unexpected 4xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
PlausibleNotFoundError = PlausibleNotFound
PlausibleServerError   = PlausibleNetworkError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request_with_retry`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; otherwise exponential backoff `0.5 * 2^attempt + jitter` capped at 16s; retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `PlausibleNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
404 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            — site misconfigured, not an auth failure
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Plausible only shows the key once at generation; rotate from **Settings → API Keys** if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401 from Plausible | Verify the key has not been revoked. Generate a new key and update the connector. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — the key lacks Stats API scope | Plausible API keys default to Stats access. Re-generate the key if scoping was changed. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` after 429 | Rate-limit hit | Default cap is 600 req/min. The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower call volume or batch metrics into single `/stats/aggregate` requests. |
| `PlausibleNotFound` on `realtime_visitors` | `default_site_id` does not match a site visible to the key | Verify the site domain in **Sites Provisioning** and update `default_site_id`. |
| Events appear to be accepted but never land | Wrong `domain` value in `record_pageview`/`record_custom_event` | The `domain` field must match the Plausible site primary domain exactly. |
| `Authorization` header rejected on `/events` | Some self-hosted instances reject Bearer on `/events` — the connector intentionally omits it; if your fork *requires* a token, fork `client/http_client.py::_event_headers`. | — |
| Self-hosted instance not reachable | `base_url` points to the wrong host or port | Set `base_url` to the full origin (e.g. `https://analytics.example.com`); the connector appends `/api/v1` automatically. |
